scenes.text: In the first scene of our lesson on JavaScript functions, we will explore the fundamental concepts of creating and using functions. A function in JavaScript is a block of code designed to perform a particular task, which is executed when it is invoked. Functions allow you to organize your code into reusable blocks, making it more modular and easier to maintain. We will discuss the basics of function declaration, how to call functions, and the importance of passing parameters to make functions more dynamic.

In the second scene of our lesson, we introduce the concept of higher-order functions in JavaScript. These are functions that take other functions as arguments or return them as output, allowing for more abstract and flexible code handling. Higher-order functions are a powerful feature of JavaScript, facilitating the creation of more expressive and concise code. We will look into examples of higher-order functions, such as `map`, `filter`, and `reduce`, which are commonly used for array processing and functional programming in JavaScript.
